children.heading: Authorization and availability
children.label: a Authorization and availability
children.text: There is authorized a separate fund in the Treasury of the United States to be known as the Upper Colorado River Basin Fund (hereinafter referred to as the Basin Fund), which shall remain available until expended, as hereafter provided, for carrying out provisions of this chapter other than .section 620g of this title

children.heading: Crediting of appropriations
children.label: b Crediting of appropriations
children.text: All appropriations made for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of this chapter, other than shall be credited to the Basin Fund as advances from the general fund of the Treasury.section 620g of this title

children.heading: Crediting and availability of revenues
children.label: c Crediting and availability of revenues
children.text: All revenues collected in connection with the operation of the Colorado River storage project and participating projects shall be credited to the Basin Fund, and shall be available, without further appropriation, for (1) defraying the costs of operation, maintenance, and replacements of, and emergency expenditures for, all facilities of the Colorado River storage project and participating projects, within such separate limitations as may be included in annual appropriation acts: , That with respect to each participating project, such costs shall be paid from revenues received from each such project; (2) payment as required by subsection (d) of this section; and (3) payment as required by subsection (e) of this section. Revenues credited to the Basin Fund shall not be available for appropriation for construction of the units and participating projects authorized by or pursuant to this chapter.Provided

children.heading: Payments of revenues in excess of operating needs to Treasury
children.label: d Payments of revenues in excess of operating needs to Treasury

children.text: the costs of each unit, participating project, or any separable feature thereof which are allocated to power pursuant to , within a period not exceeding fifty years from the date of completion of such unit, participating project, or separable feature thereof;section 620e of this title

the costs of each unit, participating project, or any separable feature thereof which are allocated to municipal water supply pursuant to , within a period not exceeding fifty years from the date of completion of such unit, participating project, or separable feature thereof;section 620e of this title

interest on the unamortized balance of the investment (including interest during construction) in the power and municipal water supply features of each unit, participating project, or any separable feature thereof, at a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury as provided in subsection (f), and interest due shall be a first charge;
the costs of each storage unit which are allocated to irrigation pursuant to within a period not exceeding fifty years; andsection 620e of this title

the costs of each salinity control unit or separable feature thereof, the costs of measures to replace incidental fish and wildlife values foregone, and the costs of the on-farm measures payable from the Upper Colorado River Basin Fund in accordance with sections 1595(a)(2), 1595(a)(3), and 1595(c) of this title.

children.heading: Apportionment of excess revenues among States
children.label: e Apportionment of excess revenues among States
children.text: Revenues in the Basin Fund in excess of the amounts needed to meet the requirements of clause (1) of subsection (c) of this section, and to return to the general fund of the Treasury the costs set out in subsection (d) of this section, shall be apportioned among the States of the Upper Division in the following percentages: Colorado, 46 per centum; Utah, 21.5 per centum; Wyoming, 15.5 per centum; and New Mexico, 17 per centum: , That prior to the application of such percentages, all revenues remaining in the Basin Fund from each participating project (or part thereof), herein or hereafter authorized, after payments, where applicable, with respect to such projects, to the general fund of the Treasury under subparagraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (d) of this section shall be apportioned to the State in which such participating project, or part thereof, is located.ProvidedRevenues so apportioned to each State shall be used only for the repayment of construction costs of participating projects or parts of such projects in the State to which such revenues are apportioned and shall not be used for such purpose in any other State without the consent, as expressed through its legally constituted authority, of the State to which such revenues are apportioned. Subject to such requirement, there shall be paid annually into the general fund of the Treasury from the revenues apportioned to each State (1) the costs of each participating project herein authorized (except Paonia) or any separable feature thereof, which are allocated to irrigation pursuant to , within a period not exceeding fifty years, in addition to any development period authorized by law, from the date of completion of such participating project or separable feature thereof, or, in the case of Indian lands, payment in accordance with ; (2) costs of the Paonia project, which are beyond the ability of the water users to repay, within a period prescribed in the Act of (); and (3) costs in connection with the irrigation features of the Eden project as specified in the Act of ().section 620e of this titlesection 620c of this title61 Stat. 18163 Stat. 277June 25, 1947June 28, 1949

children.heading: Determination of interest rate
children.label: f Determination of interest rate
children.text: The interest rate applicable to each unit of the storage project and each participating project for purposes of computing interest during construction and interest on the unpaid balance shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, as of the beginning of the fiscal year in which construction is initiated, on the basis of the computed average interest rate payable by the Treasury upon its outstanding marketable public obligations, which are neither due nor callable for redemption for fifteen years from the date of issue.

children.heading: Budget to be submitted to Congress
children.label: g Budget to be submitted to Congress
children.text: Business-type budgets shall be submitted to the Congress annually for all operations financed by the Basin Fund.
